claims.verification: Both exact guidance records describe a self-limiting course around one week, with Healthdirect allowing up to about 10 days. Direction is no_clear_effect because this is natural history rather than treatment benefit.
limitations: Guideline-level natural-history statement; cause- and population-specific trajectories remain heterogeneous.

claims.verification: Healthdirect presents gargling as self-care custom; NICE found no qualifying mouthwash trials. The claim explicitly reports uncertain comfort rather than proven symptom or duration effect.
limitations: No qualifying direct trial was included; absence of identified evidence is not evidence of no effect.

removedClaims.reason: Combined paracetamol and ibuprofen despite different directness; underlying trial, harms and special-population extraction was insufficient for review-ready mapping.

Combined local anaesthetic, NSAID and antiseptic formulations. Formulation-specific reviews were retained in research, but sponsorship, Australian applicability and absolute-effect extraction remain incomplete.
